name: PUMPKIN AND PEANUT SOUP

ingredients.item: mushrooms (straw, portobello, enoki, or white), stems trimmed and rinsed in salted water
vegetable or mushroom stock powder, plus more if needed
raw peanuts with skin, soaked in room-temperature water for a few hours
pumpkin (see Note), peeled, seeds removed, and cut into 2-inch (5 cm) cubes
Few sprigs sawtooth herb or cilantro, finely chopped, for garnish

method: Cut smaller mushrooms in half and larger ones into quarters.
In a small bowl, combine mushrooms, minced leek, stock powder, and pepper. Mix well and set aside for about 15 minutes.
Remove the silky skin of the raw peanuts and roughly crush them with a mortar and pestle. (Alternatively, you can put them in a resealable plastic bag and crush them with a rolling pin).
In a medium saucepan, heat oil over medium heat. Add the mushroom-leek mixture and cook and stir until tender (2 to 3 minutes). Transfer contents of saucepan to a bowl.
Add the water to the same saucepan, along with salt and peanuts, and cook, occasionally skimming off foam, until the peanuts are quite soft (about 15 minutes).
Add pumpkin cubes, cover, and cook over low heat until soft (about 15 minutes more). When you can easily break the cubes with a chopstick or fork, they are done.
Return the mushroom-leek mixture to the soup and season with salt and/or stock powder to taste.
Garnish with sawtooth herb or cilantro and serve hot.
